title: Strict Typing, Nullability, and Boundary Validation · Risk Brief: Boundary Validation
summary: Establish a strict type baseline without confusing static types with runtime validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates boundary validation increment in a strict type and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
Model null, missing, unknown, and unchecked indexed access explicitly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Build Boundary Validation evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-boundary-validation-in5c4d: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Boundary Validation into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-boundary-validation-in5c4d:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Boundary Validation model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Boundary Validation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
